title: The Holiday Movie Draft
format: Sean, Amanda, and Chris Ryan run the holiday edition of the movie draft: six categories — drama, comedy/action/horror, musical, Oscar nominee, blockbuster, and a wildcard opened up to any holiday content, not just movies.

segments.heading: What counts as a holiday movie
segments.note: setting the rules before the draft

films.title: Iron Man 3
films.note: Chris Ryan's running bit: 'Christmas is in every Shane Black movie.'

films.title: Thor: The Dark World
films.note: cited as the MCU movie with 'some holiday stuff' in it, short of a real Christmas movie.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: the test case for a definition stretched too far: 'if somebody tries to be like Endgame as a Christmas movie... come on, dawg.'

films.title: Jaws
films.note: Amanda's joke about the wildcard rule getting too loose: 'Fennessy can pick Jaws because it's July 4th.'

picks.title: Home Alone
picks.note: the first overall pick, over the objections of Bill Simmons, who doesn't count it as a Christmas movie.

picks.title: The Apartment
picks.note: 'won like every single Oscar ever'; spans both Christmas and New Year's, the whole holiday run.

picks.title: A Very Murray Christmas
picks.note: Sofia Coppola's Bill Murray special, filmed at the Carlyle's Bemelman's Bar; 'way underrated.'

picks.title: Miracle on 34th Street
picks.note: the 1947 original; rewatched most years, also covered on the show's best-courtroom-dramas list.

picks.title: Bridget Jones's Diary
picks.note: starts and ends at Christmas; she had a reading from it at her wedding.

picks.title: A Christmas Gift for You from Phil Spector
picks.note: not a film — the Phil Spector Christmas album, played every year 'to my husband's chagrin,' picked under the show's expanded wildcard rule.

picks.title: Die Hard
picks.note: nominated for four Oscars — visual effects, sound mixing, film editing, sound editing; 'basically invents the next 30 years of action movies.'

picks.title: Carol
picks.note: Todd Haynes; picked fresh off thinking about him via the Velvet Underground documentary.

picks.title: National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation
picks.note: the National Lampoon movie that 'found an extra gear'; Randy Quaid's supporting turn singled out.

picks.title: Meet Me in St. Louis
picks.note: Vincente Minnelli; Judy Garland's 'Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas.'

picks.title: Lethal Weapon
picks.note: set at Christmas in L.A.; 'famously features a cocaine bust in a Christmas tree lot.'

picks.title: Darlene Love's Letterman Christmas performances
picks.note: not a film — her annual 'Christmas (Baby Please Come Home)' performances on Letterman, dating back to his public-access days; the pick that inspired the wildcard category.

picks.title: It's a Wonderful Life
picks.note: Frank Capra; 'a movie about a guy losing his mind' for 98 minutes before the famous last ten.

picks.title: Elf
picks.note: Will Ferrell as Buddy, directed by Jon Favreau; 'the people's champ.'

picks.title: The Nightmare Before Christmas
picks.note: produced and story by Tim Burton, directed by Henry Selick; 75 minutes, 'not aged a day.'

picks.title: Scrooged
picks.note: Richard Donner's Christmas Carol retelling with Bill Murray, nominated for Best Makeup; Michael O'Donoghue co-wrote.

picks.title: Gremlins
picks.note: Joe Dante, produced by Steven Spielberg; made $200 million.

picks.title: Phantom Thread
picks.note: Paul Thomas Anderson's, by his count 'the best New Year's Eve movie.'

films.title: Kiss Kiss Bang Bang
films.note: Chris's Shane Black movie he couldn't find room for.

films.title: Holiday Affair
films.note: Robert Mitchum and Janet Leigh; a widow, her son, and a drifter. 'The most charming Christmas movie you've ever seen,' newly discovered on HBO Max.

films.title: Four Christmases
films.note: Vince Vaughn and Reese Witherspoon; 'an incredible Robert Duvall performance.'

films.title: Diner
films.note: Amanda's dad's pick, shown to her at age seven — 'it didn't take.' Lost the Oscar-nominee slot to The Apartment.

films.title: Trading Places
films.note: the hypothetical over Elf: 'one thousand times out of a thousand.'

films.title: Bad Santa
films.note: on both Sean and Amanda's lists, picked by neither.

films.title: The Night Before
films.note: the 'underrated' Seth Rogen Christmas movie.

films.title: Happiest Season
films.note: called out as a favorite, if 'a little new' for the mix.

films.title: The Holiday
films.note: passed over for being 'approximately 14 hours long' — one of the longest films ever put in cinemas, by Sean's count.

films.title: Love Actually
films.note: expected of Sean, not picked.

films.title: Black Christmas
films.note: Sean wasn't sure horror belonged in the conversation at all.

films.title: Silent Night, Deadly Night
films.note: 'a pretty good slasher,' Chris's counter-suggestion.

films.title: The Family Stone
films.note: Amanda expected it of Chris; 'that movie's insane' — Sarah Jessica Parker, Dermot Mulroney, Claire Danes, Luke Wilson.

films.title: The Lion in Winter
films.note: another family-destroys-itself-at-Christmas favorite, from an older period.

films.title: Metropolitan
films.note: Whit Stillman's, floated as a good one.

films.title: A Christmas Story
films.note: 'a classic that was never a classic' to either Sean or Chris.

films.title: Home for the Holidays
films.note: floated with barely a reaction: 'have you seen that one? Not really.'

films.title: Fanny and Alexander
films.note: Bergman, 'arguably his greatest film,' still on the board for Oscar nominee when Sean passed on it.

films.title: Batman Returns
films.note: also on the board for Oscar nominee — 'a terrific Christmas movie,' unpicked.

films.title: Eight Crazy Nights
films.note: the most well-known Hanukkah movie, brought up since none got picked.

films.title: The Hebrew Hammer
films.note: floated as another Hanukkah option; nobody at the table had seen it.

films.title: Planes, Trains and Automobiles
films.note: the Thanksgiving movie that's 'in the rearview mirror' by December.

films.title: Call Me by Your Name
films.note: debated as a maybe — 'most of it's about the summer.'

films.title: An American Tail
films.note: Fievel, brought up as a Gizmo cousin.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: Amanda's biggest regret — 'could have been a first overall pick,' either version.

films.title: Eyes Wide Shut
films.note: 'definitely' a Christmas movie by the group's own definition, surprisingly unpicked.

films.title: The Sound of Music
films.note: Chris watches it every Christmas but self-excluded it: 'it's not about the holidays, it's just played on TV every year at that time.'

films.title: When Harry Met Sally...
films.note: Sean's actual pick for best New Year's Eve movie, over Phantom Thread.

films.title: 200 Cigarettes
films.note: Sean's other New Year's Eve nominee.

TV: The Year Without a Santa Claus
The Sex Lives of College Girls
